title: Listening - Part 4: Working Abroad
description: You will hear five short extracts in which people talk about their experiences of living and working in a foreign country. You must complete two tasks while listening.

content.audio_script: Speaker 1: I’d always been a bit of a homebody, so heading to Tokyo was a massive leap for me. [21]The primary motivation was simply that my career had hit a bit of a brick wall at home[/21], and I needed a fresh challenge to spice up my CV. It was tough at first, especially the language barrier. However, [26]what I really didn't expect was how much my confidence would soar[/26]. Back home, I’d hesitate to lead a meeting, but navigating a completely different corporate culture has made me realise I’m far more capable than I gave myself credit for. || Speaker 2: I was headhunted for a position in Berlin, and since I’d always admired German engineering, I jumped at the chance. [22]To be honest, the salary package was so lucrative I would have been foolish to turn it down[/22]. It’s allowed me to save for a house in a way that wouldn’t have been possible otherwise. The best part, though, isn't the money. [27]It’s the fact that I’ve managed to pick up the language almost fluently[/27]. I never thought I had a gift for tongues, but being forced to use it every day at the baker's and the office has been incredibly rewarding. || Speaker 3: My move to Mexico City was purely about [23]escaping the monotonous routine I’d fallen into[/23]. I felt like every day was a carbon copy of the last. I wanted heat, colour, and a bit of chaos. It’s been an eye-opener. Surprisingly, the professional side has been great too, but [28]it’s the lifelong friendships I’ve forged that I treasure most[/28]. There’s a group of us from all over the world, and we’ve become a sort of surrogate family. I think those bonds will last long after I eventually move on. || Speaker 4: I’ve been teaching in rural Thailand for a year. [24]I initially went because I felt a strong urge to do something that actually benefited a community[/24], rather than just making shareholders richer. It's been humbling. Life is basic, but [29]the slower pace of life has done wonders for my stress levels[/29]. In Sydney, I was constantly checking my emails and rushing to the next appointment. Here, I’ve finally learned how to just 'be' and appreciate the present moment without feeling guilty about not being productive. || Speaker 5: Moving to Toronto was a strategic move. [25]I wanted to be at the heart of the tech scene where all the major innovations are happening[/25]. If you want to be the best, you have to work with the best. It’s been intense, and the winter was brutal, but [30]I’ve developed a much broader perspective on global issues[/30]. Being in such a multicultural hub, you hear viewpoints that you just aren't exposed to in a smaller, more homogenous city. It’s really challenged my underlying assumptions about the world.

task1.title: Task One
task1.instruction: For questions 21 – 25, choose from the list (A – H) the main reason each speaker gives for moving abroad.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 mentions that their career had 'hit a brick wall' at home and needed a challenge.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 admits the 'salary package was so lucrative' it would be foolish to refuse.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 wanted to escape a 'monotonous routine' where every day was a 'carbon copy'.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 wanted to do something that 'benefited a community' rather than making profit.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 wanted to be 'at the heart of the tech scene' for major innovations.

options.E: to find a change of pace

task2.title: Task Two
task2.instruction: For questions 26 – 30, choose from the list (A – H) what each speaker has gained most from the experience.

questions.speaker: Speaker 1
questions.explanation: Speaker 1 says their 'confidence would soar' and they feel more capable.

questions.speaker: Speaker 2
questions.explanation: Speaker 2 mentions picking up the language 'almost fluently' as the best part.

questions.speaker: Speaker 3
questions.explanation: Speaker 3 treasures the 'lifelong friendships' they have forged.

questions.speaker: Speaker 4
questions.explanation: Speaker 4 appreciates the 'slower pace of life' and reduced stress.

questions.speaker: Speaker 5
questions.explanation: Speaker 5 says they have developed a 'much broader perspective on global issues'.
